title: ICAI Brings PM Internship Scheme for CA Firms & Eases Advertising Rules
content:
# ICAI Brings PM Internship Scheme for CA Firms & Eases Advertising Rules

Ek important update hai for all CA professionals and students! The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) ne recently two significant initiatives announce kiye hain: a Prime Minister Internship Scheme for CA firms and revised advertising guidelines for its members. These moves aim to strengthen the profession and create more opportunities.

### Key Updates:

*   **PM Internship Scheme for CA Firms:**
    *   ICAI is set to facilitate the participation of CA firms in the Pradhan Mantri Internship Scheme (PMIS).
    *   Under this scheme, interns joining CA firms will receive a monthly stipend of at least ₹9,000.
    *   The Central Government will contribute ₹8,100, while the remaining ₹900 or more can be paid by CA firms from non-Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) funds. This addresses the previous hurdle where CA firms typically do not maintain CSR funds.
    *   The scheme intends to provide practical exposure in accounting, auditing, taxation, compliance, and advisory services to students.
    *   At least 50% of the internship period is expected to be spent in actual work/real-life business environments.

*   **Revised Advertising Guidelines (Code of Ethics – 13th Edition):**
    *   ICAI has operationalized its revised Code of Ethics, which includes significant relaxations in advertisement and website guidelines for Chartered Accountants and their firms.
    *   These revised guidelines are effective from April 1, 2026.
    *   Firms can now publish advertisements detailing their services, subject to ICAI's professional standards and guidelines.
    *   The new rules allow greater flexibility for a professional online presence, including using "structured write-ups" to reach prospective clients and digital promotion for non-exclusive services like consultancy or tax advisory.
    *   However, the core principle of prohibiting direct client solicitation remains. There are still restrictions on using laudatory language, client testimonials, comparative claims, or promotional offers.
    *   The updated Code also strengthens independence requirements for non-assurance services provided to audit clients and introduces new ethics and independence standards for sustainability assurance.

These initiatives, announced by ICAI President CA Prasanna Kumar D, are aimed at modernizing the profession and enhancing opportunities for students and firms alike.

## Read the Official Document
* [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.icai.org/new_category.html?id=101918)

*Published on: 10 June 2026*
